entry.title: How Much Does It Really Cost to Build on SAP BTP ABAP Environment? A Practical Guide for Partners
entry.content:
When partners evaluate SAP BTP ABAP Environment, one of the first questions is simple: What does it actually cost to get started?

With the introduction of 0.5 ABAP Compute Units (ACU), the answer has become much more attractive.

Instead of starting at 1 ACU, partners can now begin with 0.5 ACU (8 GB runtime memory), making development, test and demo landscapes significantly more cost-efficient.

At the same time, SAP BTP ABAP Environment supports two delivery options:

  • Multitenant SaaS solution – operated by the partner
  • Add-On Product – installed and operated by the customer

Both options can be combined depending on your business model.

 

Commercial Model for Partners

To build and commercialize solutions on SAP BTP ABAP Environment, partners operate within the SAP PartnerEdge – Build (PE Build) commercial model.

This model provides the foundation for:

  • Developing and running solutions
  • Delivering them as multitenant SaaS or Add-On Products
  • Publishing offerings via the SAP Store

 

A Typical Starting Point

A realistic setup for partners usually includes a small development landscape.

Development & Demo (TDD PAYG)

  • Development system → 0.5 ACU
  • Test/Demo system → 0.5 ACU
  • Persistence → 2 HCU per system

This setup is typically used for:

  • Development
  • Testing
  • Customer demos and workshops

 

Cost Breakdown (TDD PAYG)

All following calculations are based on the Pay-As-You-Go for SAP BTP for cloud test, demo, and development for SAP BTP ABAP Environment. Please also refer to SAP BTP Pricing Options for more details and an online cost estimator. 

Pricing basis:

  • Runtime: 0.48 € / hour per 1 ACU (16 GB)
  • For this example, we use 0.5 ACU → 0.24 € / hour
  • Persistence: 0.10 € / hour per block (16 GB)
  • Minimum persistence: 2 blocks → 0.20 € / hour

For the latest pricing details, refer to the SAP BTP Discovery Center.

Monthly Costs per System (0.5 ACU, 2 HCU)

Component Calculation Monthly Costs
Runtime0.24 € × 730 h~175 €
Persistence0.20 € × 730 h~145 €
Total per system ~320 €

For the latest pricing details, refer to the SAP BTP Discovery Center.

What This Looks Like in Practice

A simple Dev and Demo setup results in:

System Monthly Cost
Development (0.5 ACU + 2 HCU)~320 €
Test / Demo (0.5 ACU + 2 HCU)~320 €
Total~640 € / month

This is your starting point without any optimization.

Reducing Costs with System Hibernation

For non-productive systems, System Hibernation provides an effective way to reduce costs when using a consumption-based commercial model. See this blog post for more information.

When a system is stopped:

  • Runtime costs (ACU) → 0%
  • Persistence costs (HCU) → 12.5%

Example Usage Pattern

  • Active: 12 hours per day on 20 working days
  • Stopped: nights and weekends

Resulting Monthly Cost per System (0.5 ACU, 2 HCU)

Active usage (runtime + persistence)~106 €
Hibernation (reduced persistence)~11 €
Total per system~116 € / month

Development & Demo Landscape

System monthly costs
Development~116 €
Test / Demo~116 €
Total~233 € / month

This shows how significantly costs can be reduced when systems are only used during working hours.

 

Going Live Option 1: Adding a Production System (Multitenant SaaS)

If you operate your solution as a SaaS offering, you typically add a production system:

  • Runtime → 1 ACU
  • Persistence → 4 HCU (recommended)

Production systems are typically sized more conservatively than development systems to ensure performance and availability.

Pricing (PE Build)

Material description price (€ / month)
8006181SAP BTP ABAP environment runtime (16 GB)635.46 €
8006182SAP BTP ABAP environment persistence (16 GB)111.94 €

Persistence requires 4 units:

  • 4 × 111.94 € = 447.76 €

Total production cost: ~1083 € / month

Full SaaS Landscape Example

Development~320 €
Test / Demo~320 €
Production~1083 €
Total~1,723 € / month
 
 

Going Live Option 2:  Add-On Product Scenario

If you deliver your solution as an Add-On Product:

  • You only need development and test systems
  • The customer operates the production system

Result:

  • No production infrastructure cost on the partner side
  • Lower operational responsibility
  • Developer extensibility for customer
  • Flexible delivery model, especially for enterprise customers

 

Free SAP Build TDD Capacity for SAP Partners

SAP currently offers free test, demo, and development (TDD) capacity as part of SAP Build runtime.

This includes:

cloud test, demo, and development for SAP Build runtime

More information:

https://partneredge.sap.com/en/partnership/licenses/wp_payg_btp.html
https://partneredge.sap.com/en/products/sap-bld/about/sap_build_tdd.html

To get started:

  • Order “Pay-As-You-Go for SAP BTP for cloud test, demo, and development” (Material 8009282)
  • Activate services in the SAP BTP cockpit

What This Means in Practice

In practice, this helps you significantly reduce costs for development and demo scenarios.

A large part of the runtime is covered by the included quota so you mainly pay for persistence and any usage beyond the free limit.

If the quota is exceeded, usage continues seamlessly via PAYG.

In many cases, the free quota can cover the runtime of development and demo systems, while persistence costs remain.

What Happens Beyond the Free Limit

If the fair use limit is exceeded:

  • Consumption continues seamlessly
  • Billing automatically switches to PAYG pricing

 

Summary: What It Actually Takes to Get Started

Estimated monthly cost per scenario - choose one these options:
Dev + Demo (no hibernation)0.5 ACU, 2 HCU~640 €
Dev + Demo (with hibernation)0.5 ACU, 2 HCU, working hours only~233 €
Full SaaS landscape (no hibernation)Dev + Demo + Production (1 ACU + 4 HCU)~1,720 €
Full SaaS landscape (with hibernation)Dev + Demo + Production (1 ACU + 4 HCU)~1,310 €
Add-On model (partner side only)Dev + Demo with hibernation~233 €

 

Recommended Approach for New Partners

  • Start with 0.5 ACU systems for development and demos
  • Enable System Hibernation to reduce costs
  • Use the free TDD quota during the initial phase
  • Choose the delivery model based on your business model
  • Add a production system only when you are ready to go live

entry.title: From RAP to Mobile: Reusing OData V4 Services with SAP Mobile Services and MDK
entry.content:
When building applications with the RESTful ABAP Programming Model (RAP), the same business logic often needs to serve different consumers: desktop users via Fiori apps and mobile users via lightweight applications.

In this post, we demonstrate how to reuse a single RAP Business Object and expose it as two optimized OData V4 services:

  • A draft-enabled service for Fiori Elements (desktop)
  • A lightweight, non-draft service for mobile apps using SAP Mobile Services and MDK

This approach allows you to keep one consistent backend while tailoring the API to the needs of each consumer.

Step 1 – Create or Reuse a RAP Business Object

Start by creating a RAP Business Object or reusing an existing one.

In this example, we used the OData UI Service from Scratch Generator to generate a complete RAP application from an empty package. This includes:

  • CDS views
  • Behavior definitions
  • Service definition and binding
  • Fiori Elements UI

Step 2 – Create a Mobile-Specific Projection (Without Draft)

For mobile scenarios, draft handling is often unnecessary and adds complexity.

Instead of modifying the existing BO, create a separate projection layer:

  • ZC_EVENT_M (mobile projection)
  • Based on the same root entity
  • Without draft behavior

Example behavior definition:

projection;
strict (2);

define behavior for ZC_EVENT_M alias Event
{
  use create;
  use update;
  use delete;
}

This provides a clean CRUD API without draft semantics. This avoids unnecessary draft handling in mobile scenarios and simplifies the API consumption in MDK.

Step 3 – Expose a Dedicated Mobile Service

We created a separate service for mobile consumption:

  • Service Definition: ZUI_EVENT_MOBILE
  • Service Binding: ZUI_EVENT_MOBILE_O4

define service ZUI_EVENT_MOBILE {
  expose ZC_EVENT_M as Event;
}

This ensures:

  • No draft endpoints
  • Lightweight API for mobile apps

Step 4 – Add UI Annotations for Mobile Preview

Without dedicated UI annotations, the MDK wizard may not generate the expected columns – either showing too many fields or missing the intended selection and order.

Reason:

  • UI annotations were only defined for the Fiori projection (ZC_EVENT000)
  • The mobile service exposes ZC_EVENT_M

Solution:

  • Create a separate Metadata Extension for ZC_EVENT_M

Example:

annotate view ZC_EVENT_M with
{
  @ui.lineItem: [{ position: 10 }]
  EventID;

  @ui.lineItem: [{ position: 20 }]
  EventName;

  @ui.lineItem: [{ position: 30 }]
  DateFrom;

@ui.lineItem: [{ position: 30 }]
  DateTo;
}

After this step, the mobile preview correctly displays the list report.

 

Step 5 – Backend Communication Setup

On the backend, create the communication scenario and communication arrangement to expose your OData service.

This step provides the service URL, which will be consumed from SAP BTP.

Step 6 – Create the SAP BTP Destination

Next, create a destination in SAP BTP pointing to your RAP OData service.

Configure the destination according to your authentication setup (e.g. OAuth 2.0 / token exchange).

This acts as the connection layer between RAP and Mobile Services.

Step 7 – Create the Mobile Services Application

Create a new Mobile Services application connected to your destination.

Before proceeding further, test your OData service here.

This is a crucial checkpoint to ensure:

  • the destination works
  • authentication is correct
  • the service is reachable
  • metadata loads successfully

Testing here early avoids debugging issues later in MDK.

Step 8 – Build the MDK App in BAS

In SAP Business Application Studio, create a new project:

New Project from Template → MDK

When selecting the template type, choose CRUD.

This is important because:

  • the CRUD template enables create, update, and delete operations
  • otherwise, the app may behave as read-only

From here, you can:

  • bind the app to your OData service
  • define pages and navigation
  • implement mobile-specific UI logic

Architecture Overview

Fiori App Preview:

 

Mobile App:

Lessons Learned

The most challenging part was getting the OData service to work smoothly within the MDK application. Additional OData vocabulary references generated by the RAP system caused issues in the MDK app.

These references had to be ignored in the CDS configuration, which took some time to identify.

Key Takeaways

  • RAP allows you to reuse one BO for multiple channels
  • Draft is useful for Fiori, but often not required for mobile
  • A separate projection + service is a clean design for mobile scenarios
  • UI annotations must match the exposed entity
  • Always test your service in Mobile Services before MDK development
  • Use the MDK CRUD template to enable full interaction

entry.title: ABAP AI - Chapter 3: We go agentic!
entry.content:
Summary

  • SAP Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities will be supported in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition in all releases as of 2021 with planned start in Q2/2026
  • SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities will be priced on a consumption basis via AI Units
  • ABAP Development Tools for VS Code are planned to be GA in Q2/2026
  • An ABAP MCP Server will be shipped in ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse and ABAP Development Tools for VS Code as of Q2/2026 enabling agentic ABAP development experience
  • An ABAP Custom Code Transformation agent focusing on the migration from SAP ECC towards SAP S/4HANA is planned for Q2/2026

Intro

Since the start of ABAP AI in early 2025 (“Joule speaks ABAP!”), the update in September 2025 (“ABAP AI – Chapter 2”), now it is time to deliver on the promises made at SAP TechEd 2025 (“Introducing the next era of ABAP development”) – time for chapter 3!

We are entering the agentic era of ABAP development: let's get started.

SAP Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities in lower releases

As of June 2026, Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities are offered through a running on SAP BTP. This hub service combines authentication, license check, access to SAP BTP AI Core and use case specific business logic and can be plugged directly to the developer’s IDE. This way, we can offer ABAP AI capabilities mostly independent of the ABAP backend release. This enables us to serve SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition on releases 2021 and higher with Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities.

  • Planned availability in Q2/26
  • Supported releases: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2021 and higher
  • Costs: See below
  • Configuration guide and further information will follow and will be linked here

New commercial approach for ABAP AI

The free promotion for J4D ABAP AI capabilities comes to an end in September 2026. Accordingly, it is now time to launch the commercial model: Based on AI units, ABAP AI capabilities are now priced purely based on consumption. This approach combines transparency regarding the real costs based on the usage and is well-suited for dynamic and individual agentic AI use cases.

  • Planned availability in Q2/26
  • Materials: 8019164 (customers) and 8019670 (partners)
  • Information on how to purchase and activate will follow soon and will be linked here

ABAP Development Tools for VS Code

14 years after the launch of ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse, time has come to support a new IDE. As Q2 2026, ABAP Development Tools will be available for VS Code. Building on the ABAP Language Server (see “Behind the Design…”), coupled with an ABAP MCP Server, this is the enabler for a truly agentic ABAP development experience, boosting development efficiency to the next level. It sets the foundation to leverage modern coding agents like GitHub Copilot directly for ABAP development. While we start with a small set of development objects and IDE tools focusing on SAP Fiori App development based on the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP), we will very soon enhance the scope of object types to serve high priority development scenarios.

The VS Code extension is

  • planned to be available as of Q2/26
  • Supported releases: release 7.31 and higher
  • Works in the Cloud and OnPrem
  • Costs: free of charge
  • Precise info on how to obtain and get started will follow soon and will be linked here

MCP Support for ABAP development

The Language Server provides the required abstraction to gain independence of the IDE sitting on top. A major component of this delivery is the MCP server for ABAP development. It provides powerful tools which help coding agents to perform complex ABAP coding tasks. While the Language Server is currently delivered as part of the ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse and for VS Code, a potential standalone-shipment of the Language and MCP Server is under discussion.

  • Release planned for Q2/26
  • Supported in Eclipse and VS Code
  • Works in the Cloud and OnPrem
  • Supported for release 7.31 and higher
  • Precise info on how to use it in the IDE will follow soon and will be linked here

Agentic custom code migration for the SAP S/4HANA transformation

Bringing all pieces together, we are launching the first ABAP custom code agent assisting in the transformation of custom code from ECC towards SAP S/4HANA. The required capabilities are offered through the ABAP Language and MCP Server explained above and can be used leveraging a coding agent, for example Amazon Q or GitHub Copilot. Currently, Eclipse must be used for this agent, until all required ABAP object types are supported in VS Code (see above). This agent boosts our customers on the RISE journey when migrating their classic landscape to the Cloud ERP.

  • Availability planned for Q2/26.
  • Supported in Eclipse
  • Prerequisites: Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities via the new commercial approach with AI units (see above) and coding agent on top (e.g. Amazon Q or Github Copilot)
  • Further information to follow soon and will be linked here

 

Finally - please have a look at our FAQ document for frequent questions and watch out for the GA releases of these deliverables. We will keep you posted.

Get yourself ready to dive into agentic ABAP development! 😎
